superstarBaseballCodes.intro: Codes are the fastest way to get extra resources in Superstar Baseball. Redemption happens from the in-game settings menu.

redeemSteps: Scroll to the bottom of the settings panel.
Paste a code into the redemption box.
Press Enter to receive the reward.

superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Beginner Guide
superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.subtitle: How to Start Playing Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.intro: Superstar Baseball is a Roblox sports game by Metavision. New players begin by rolling styles, learning the three core on-field roles, and getting comfortable with the pitch mix before focusing on optimization.

steps.title: Enter the Game
steps.description: Search for Superstar Baseball on Roblox and join a server. The early experience is built around learning your style setup and basic match flow.

steps.title: Use Your Starting Spins
steps.description: New players begin with 4 free spins. These are used to roll one offensive style and one defensive style.

steps.title: Understand Spin Odds
steps.description: A free spin is granted every 30 minutes. Reported style odds are Legendary 0.5%, Epic 2%, Rare 35%, and Uncommon 62.5%.

steps.title: Learn the Three Roles
steps.description: Fielder catches the ball and throws for outs. Pitcher tries to miss bats or force weak contact. Batter aims to clear the infield and bring runners home.

steps.title: Know the Main Pitch Types
steps.description: Curveball runs about 65-85 mph, Sinker about 70-90 mph, Slider about 75-90 mph, and Fastball about 80-100 mph.

steps.title: Focus on Simple Early Wins
steps.description: Start by learning swing timing, recognizing pitch speed, making clean throws as a fielder, and saving better resources for stronger roster upgrades.

quickTips: A free spin resets every 30 minutes — collect it consistently.
Start with any role to learn the basics before specializing.
Fastballs arrive fastest, so react earlier than you think.
Save Lucky Spins for higher rarity style targets.

superstarBaseballControls.title: Superstar Baseball PC Controls
superstarBaseballControls.subtitle: Superstar Baseball Controls Quick Reference
superstarBaseballControls.intro: The control set in Superstar Baseball is compact, but each role uses inputs differently. Batters need fast reaction timing, pitchers need quick pitch selection, and fielders need clean dives and throws.

controls.description: Use this to hit the ball at the plate.
Chooses Curveball as the current pitch.
Chooses Sinker as the current pitch.
Chooses Slider as the current pitch.
Chooses Fastball as the current pitch.
Dive to try to secure catches and save hits.
Throw after fielding the ball and line up the green arrow with the target teammate.

superstarBaseballBattingGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Batting Guide
superstarBaseballBattingGuide.subtitle: Hit More Consistently at the Plate
superstarBaseballBattingGuide.intro: Batting in Superstar Baseball is built around reading the ball early and syncing your swing to the pitch path. Better hitters identify pitch type, wait for the right zone, and avoid wasting swings on bad balls.

steps.title: Time Your Swing Mid-Flight
steps.description: Start the swing when the ball is about halfway to the plate. Swinging too early often produces fouls, while swinging too late leads to whiffs.

steps.title: Read the Pitch Type Early
steps.description: Fastballs travel straight and need an earlier reaction. Curves break later and usually need more patience. Off-speed pitches look fast at first but arrive later.

steps.title: Aim Center-High for Power
steps.description: For stronger launch angle and home run contact, the center-high zone is the most useful target. Repeated grounders usually mean the swing is late or the contact point is too low.

steps.title: Do Not Swing at Everything
steps.description: Plate discipline matters. Letting bad pitches pass can put you on base with a walk and forces pitchers to throw more hittable balls.

steps.title: Build Around Your Best Hitter
steps.description: Slot your strongest hitter into the 3rd or 4th batting position, where RBI chances show up more often.

superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Pitching Guide
superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.subtitle: How to Pitch Smarter in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.intro: Superstar Baseball gives pitchers four core pitch types: Curveball, Sinker, Slider, and Fastball. Good pitching is built around sequencing — use early pitches to set expectations, then break the pattern with a different speed or movement shape. Corners of the zone are especially valuable.

items.title: Fastball
items.description: The straightest and fastest option. Use it to challenge late batters, establish pace early, or set up a slower follow-up pitch.
highlights: Best used when the batter is behind on timing
Pairs well with a later breaking ball
Most effective when located on the edges instead of center zone

items.title: Curveball
items.description: A slower breaking pitch that drops as it reaches the plate. Useful after faster looks, especially when the hitter is starting too early.
highlights: Strong change-of-speed option
Useful after one or two harder pitches
Good for disrupting aggressive swing timing

items.title: Sinker
items.description: Drops downward and is designed to produce weaker contact. A practical pitch when you want ground-ball results instead of pure swing-and-miss.
highlights: Designed to induce lower contact
Works well low in the zone
Useful when you want safer contact management

items.title: Slider
items.description: Breaks laterally with some downward action and is meant to deceive hitters. One of the best pattern-breakers once a batter starts reading straight velocity.
highlights: Sharp break makes timing harder
Good third-pitch choice after straighter pitches
Especially useful once a batter starts sitting fastball

items.title: Sequencing Rules That Actually Work
items.description: The strongest sequences are built around mix, location, and timing reads. A common flow: hard pitch first, another familiar look second, then a different movement or slower ball to finish.
highlights: Do not repeat the same pitch pattern every at-bat
Corners beat raw speed when placement is clean
If the hitter swings early, slow down; if late, speed up

superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Spins Guide
superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.subtitle: Spins, Lucky Spins, Style Spins, and Coins in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.intro: Superstar Baseball's progression loop revolves around several reward types. Regular Spins are common, Lucky Spins are higher-value pulls, Style Spins are cosmetic-focused, and Coins support store purchases. Codes remain the fastest way to build a starter stockpile.

items.title: Spins
items.description: The most common roll currency. Active codes like DEADEYE (5 Spins), 90KLIKES (2 Spins), and SUBZERO (10 Spins) give you a head start without grinding.
highlights: Core reward type from active codes
Used far more freely than Lucky Spins
Good first resource to spend before premium rolls

items.title: Lucky Spins
items.description: Lucky Spins are the higher-value version of regular Spins, widely recommended for rarer pulls. Codes like SOFTBALL (5 Lucky Spins) and TUTORIAL (3 Lucky Spins) deliver them directly.
highlights: Higher odds for rarer styles
Best saved for stronger upgrade attempts
Often the most valuable code reward

items.title: Style Spins
items.description: Style Spins are used for cosmetic-style rewards. Codes like 40KLIKES (2 Style Spins) and SLUGGER (5 Style Spins) give you access without spending coins.
highlights: Cosmetic-focused reward path
Useful for appearance customization
Less urgent than Lucky Spins for progression-first players

items.title: Coins
items.description: Coins are the direct purchase currency used in the in-game store. The code SUPERSTAR gives 100 Coins — a solid early boost for store purchases.
highlights: Used for purchases rather than rolling
Helpful early when combined with code rewards
Best treated as support currency beside spins

items.title: Best Early-Game Spin Plan
items.description: For a fresh account, the strongest route is to redeem all active codes first, spend standard Spins before premium ones, and hold Lucky Spins for your more important upgrade windows.
highlights: Redeem codes before grinding matches
Standard Spins are easiest to spend first
Lucky Spins are the most strategic resource to save

superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.title: Superstar Baseball Offensive Style Rarity
superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.subtitle: Offensive Style Rarity and Roll Priorities in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.intro: Players roll for offensive style slots using Spins, and reliable guides agree on four rarity bands: Legendary, Epic, Rare, and Uncommon. Lucky Spins improve access to rarer outcomes. The rarity ladder tells players what to chase, what to keep, and what to replace first.

tiers.label: Legendary
tiers.summary: Top-rarity offensive rolls. The hardest band to land and the clearest long-term chase target.

tiers.label: Epic
tiers.summary: High-rarity offensive rolls with much better accessibility than Legendary while still rare enough to value heavily.

tiers.label: Rare
tiers.summary: The practical middle band. Rare rolls are realistic progression pieces and a common place to stabilize before pushing higher.

tiers.label: Uncommon
tiers.summary: The most frequent outcome. Useful for getting started, but usually the first band players look to improve from with later spins.

superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.title: Superstar Baseball Defensive Style Rarity
superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.subtitle: Defensive Style Rarity and Upgrade Ladder in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.intro: Defensive styles use the same rarity structure as offensive ones. New players often understand batting first, but defensive value becomes more important as matches tighten up and clean plays start deciding outcomes. The rarity ladder gives players an immediate answer to what counts as a keeper roll.

tiers.label: Legendary
tiers.summary: The rarest defensive result band. These are the most desirable long-term defensive outcomes and the hardest to obtain.

tiers.label: Epic
tiers.summary: Premium defensive rolls that are still rare, but realistic enough to become strong keepers for many players.

tiers.label: Rare
tiers.summary: A strong practical tier for players who want better defense without sinking every premium roll into chasing the top band.

tiers.label: Uncommon
tiers.summary: The most common defensive outcome. Fine for starting out, but usually the first tier players move on from as more spins arrive.

superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.title: Superstar Baseball How to Hit Home Runs
superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.subtitle: Home runs come from timing first, then zone selection. This module focuses on the repeatable habits that turn clean contact into long balls.
superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.intro: Superstar Baseball rewards disciplined swings more than panic clicking. The clearest pattern across guides is to wait for hittable pitches, connect on time, and look for balls in the center-high part of the zone if you want better launch angle.

steps.title: Watch the Ball Out of the Pitcher's Hand
steps.description: Track the pitch early instead of reacting at the last second. Reading the ball out of the hand is the first part of better batting and gives you more time to judge speed and location.

steps.title: Do Not Swing at Everything
steps.description: Take balls and make the pitcher enter the zone. A walk still puts you on base, and patient at-bats create better home-run chances than chasing weak pitches.

steps.title: Start Your Swing Around the Halfway Point
steps.description: Timing is the main separator between weak contact and strong contact. Begin the swing when the ball is about halfway to the plate — swinging too early produces fouls, too late produces whiffs.

steps.title: Hunt the Center-High Part of the Strike Zone
steps.description: The most direct home-run tip is to swing at pitches in the center-high zone. That placement gives a better launch angle than low or outside contact.

steps.title: Use Ground Balls and Foul Balls as Feedback
steps.description: If you keep hitting grounders, your contact point is too low or your swing is too late. If you foul off pitches consistently, your swing is likely too early — adjust and recalibrate.

steps.title: Practice in Casual Matches Until Timing Feels Natural
steps.description: The swing window is tight, so repetition matters. Casual games are the safest place to build a feel for pitch speed and your own swing rhythm before competing seriously.

superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Fielding Guide
superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.subtitle: Fielding in Superstar Baseball is about reacting cleanly, diving at the right moment, and making accurate throws. Solid defense turns routine contact into outs.
superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.intro: The core fielding loop is clear: catch the ball, dive when needed, and throw accurately to teammates. Good defense also improves when players communicate and cover space together in 7v7 matches.

steps.title: Know Your Job as a Fielder
steps.description: The fielder role is straightforward: catch the ball and make accurate throws to get opponents out. Everything else builds on top of that core loop.

steps.title: Use the Dive When the Ball Is Just Out of Reach
steps.description: On PC, the default dive key is E. Save it for balls you cannot secure with normal movement, especially on line drives or late reactions when the ball is barely reachable.

steps.title: Secure the Catch Before Rushing the Throw
steps.description: Once you have the ball, move immediately into a clean throw instead of hesitating. Defense becomes more reliable when the catch-to-throw sequence is quick and controlled.

steps.title: Aim the Throw With the Green Arrow
steps.description: After fielding the ball, left click to throw and use the green arrow to line up the target teammate. Accurate aiming is one of the clearest documented defensive mechanics in Superstar Baseball.

steps.title: Play Team Defense in 7v7 Matches
steps.description: Superstar Baseball is built around 7v7 games, so defense is a shared responsibility. Communication and coordinated coverage make it easier to convert contact into outs consistently.

steps.title: Build Defensive Consistency Over Flashy Plays
steps.description: Stronger fielding ties directly to winning more consistently. Clean catches, better throw decisions, and fewer rushed mistakes matter more than highlight-reel plays.

superstarBaseballBestPitchTypes.title: Superstar Baseball Best Pitch Types
superstarBaseballBestPitchTypes.subtitle: Superstar Baseball has four core pitch types. Each pitch has a different speed band and movement profile — the best option depends on the batter and the count.
superstarBaseballBestPitchTypes.intro: The most useful way to compare pitch types in Superstar Baseball is by speed, movement, and what kind of contact they usually force. Fastballs pressure hitters with speed, while breaking and sinking pitches are better for deception and weak contact.

pitchRows.bestFor: Deceiving hitters and changing eye level

notes: Fastball works best when the batter is behind on timing — pair it with a slower follow-up to create an effective sequence.
Slider is useful when you want stronger movement than a fastball without dropping as much as a sinker or curveball.
Sinker is a practical choice when you want weaker contact instead of pure swing-and-miss pressure.
Curveball is the slowest of the four pitch types but one of the most effective for changing pace after faster pitches.

superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.title: Superstar Baseball How to Get More Spins
superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.subtitle: Spins are the center of Superstar Baseball progression because they unlock characters, gear, and cosmetics. The fastest routes are codes first, then passive and match-based gains.
superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.intro: Superstar Baseball uses three spin categories: Spins, Lucky Spins, and Style Spins. Start with redeemable codes, collect passive free spins, then stack match rewards and milestones for sustained progression.

items.label: Redeem All Active Codes First
items.detail: Codes are the fastest source of Spins, Lucky Spins, Style Spins, and Coins. Active examples include TUTORIAL, METRO, BASEBALLPRESENT, SUBZERO, 80KLIKES, SLUGGER, and SUPERSTAR — redeem before grinding.

items.label: Claim Lucky Spins From Milestone Codes
items.detail: Some of the most valuable code rewards are Lucky Spins, which have better odds than regular Spins. TUTORIAL, METRO, and BASEBALLPRESENT each award 3 Lucky Spins — claim these immediately on a new account.

items.label: Play Matches Consistently for Standard Spins
items.detail: Standard Spins come from match rewards and daily play. Playing games steadily is the baseline way to refill your spin pool after codes are exhausted.

items.label: Pick Up the Free Spin Every 30 Minutes
items.detail: Players receive one free spin every 30 minutes. Passive check-ins are a simple low-effort farming method that adds up significantly over time.

items.label: Hit Milestones as You Play
items.detail: Milestone progress is a way to earn more Spins over time, especially after codes are exhausted. Focus on completing objectives naturally during match play.

items.label: Spend Lucky Spins More Carefully Than Regular Spins
items.detail: Lucky Spins have better odds for rarer pulls. Save them for moments when you actually want stronger roster upgrades instead of spending them immediately on any available spin.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Superstar Baseball on Roblox

questions.question: What is Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Superstar Baseball is an anime-inspired 7v7 competitive baseball game on Roblox by Metavision. Players choose offensive and defensive styles, compete in fast-paced matches, and earn Spins and Coins as rewards.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Open Superstar Baseball, tap the settings menu, scroll to the bottom, paste a code into the redemption box, and press Enter to claim your reward.

questions.question: What are Spins in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Spins are the primary resource for rolling new styles in Superstar Baseball. A free spin is available every 30 minutes. Lucky Spins increase the chance of receiving rare or legendary styles.

questions.question: What are the three roles in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: The three roles are Batter, Pitcher, and Fielder. Batters aim to make contact and score runs. Pitchers try to generate strikeouts or weak contact. Fielders catch the ball and make throws to record outs.

questions.question: How do I get more Spins in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Spins can be earned by redeeming active codes, collecting the free spin that resets every 30 minutes, or purchasing them in the in-game store.

questions.question: Where can I play Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Superstar Baseball is available on Roblox for free. Search for Superstar Baseball on the Roblox platform or visit the official game page to start playing.
